function.arguments:
{"command": "create", "path": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/verification/scenarios/scenario_rtc_calendar.yaml", "file_text": "scenario_name: scenario_rtc_calendar
description: >
  Verifies the Real-Time Clock (RTC) of mspm33c321a_derivative. Tests cover
  BCD calendar set and read-back (seconds/minutes/hours/day/month/year),
  time advancement over one real-time second, Alarm A interrupt, Alarm B
  interrupt (independent of A), and the periodic wakeup timer interrupt.
checklist_ids:
  - RTC-01
  - RTC-02
  - RTC-03
  - RTC-04
  - RTC-05
test_names:
  - test_rtc_calendar_set_read
  - test_rtc_time_advance
  - test_rtc_alarm_a
  - test_rtc_alarm_b
  - test_rtc_periodic_wakeup
priority: P0
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

external_devices: []

rtc_params:
  rtc_clock_hz: 32768       # RTC driven by LFOSC at 32.768 kHz
  fast_sim_scale: 1000      # Speed-up factor for simulation (1 sim-second = 1000 RTC cycles)
  initial_time:
    seconds: 0x58
    minutes: 0x59
    hours:   0x23
    day:     0x28
    month:   0x02
    year:    0x26

stimulus:
  - step: 1
    action: release_reset
    description: Release reset; RTC in reset state
  - step: 2
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: RTC0
    register: ISR
    value: INIT_BIT
    description: Enter RTC init mode to allow calendar write
  - step: 3
    action: apb_write_rtc_calendar
    seconds: 0x58
    minutes: 0x59
    hours:   0x23
    day:     0x28
    month:   0x02
    year:    0x26
    description: Write BCD calendar time 23:59:58 on 28/02/2026
  - step: 4
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: RTC0
    register: ISR
    value: 0x00
    description: Exit RTC init mode; calendar starts running
  - step: 5
    action: apb_read_check_rtc
    fields: [SECONDS, MINUTES, HOURS, DAY, MONTH, YEAR]
    expected: [0x58, 0x59, 0x23, 0x28, 0x02, 0x26]
    description: Read back all calendar fields; verify BCD values match written data
  - step: 6
    action: wait_rtc_tick
    rtc_ticks: 32768
    description: Wait one RTC second (32768 LFOSC cycles in sim-scaled mode)
  - step: 7
    action: apb_read_check
    peripheral: RTC0
    register: SECONDS
    expected: 0x59
    description: Verify seconds advanced from 0x58 to 0x59
  - step: 8
    action: configure_rtc_alarm
    alarm: A
    seconds: 0x00
    minutes: 0x00
    hours:   0x00
    irq_enable: true
    description: Set Alarm A to fire at midnight (00:00:00)
  - step: 9
    action: wait_rtc_alarm_irq
    alarm: A
    irq_vector: 24
    timeout_rtc_ticks: 65536
    description: Wait for Alarm A IRQ (IRQ24) to fire when time reaches 00:00:00
  - step: 10
    action: check_irq
    irq_vector: 24
    description: Confirm Alarm A IRQ asserted
  - step: 11
    action: apb_clear_rtc_alarm_flag
    alarm: A
    description: Clear Alarm A interrupt flag
  - step: 12
    action: configure_rtc_alarm
    alarm: B
    seconds: 0x30
    minutes: 0x00
    hours:   0x00
    irq_enable: true
    description: Set Alarm B to fire at 00:00:30 (independent of Alarm A)
  - step: 13
    action: wait_rtc_alarm_irq
    alarm: B
    irq_vector: 24
    timeout_rtc_ticks: 98304
    description: Wait for Alarm B IRQ
  - step: 14
    action: check_irq
    irq_vector: 24
    description: Confirm Alarm B IRQ asserted independently
  - step: 15
    action: configure_rtc_wakeup
    interval_rtc_ticks: 8192
    irq_enable: true
    description: Configure periodic wakeup timer at 8192 RTC tick interval (~0.25 s)
  - step: 16
    action: wait_rtc_wakeup_irq
    count: 3
    irq_vector: 24
    timeout_rtc_ticks: 30000
    description: Verify wakeup IRQ fires three times at correct interval

checks:
  - id: CHK-RTC-01
    checklist_id: RTC-01
    description: All calendar fields read back matching BCD values after write
    expected: SECONDS=0x58, MINUTES=0x59, HOURS=0x23, DAY=0x28, MONTH=0x02, YEAR=0x26
  - id: CHK-RTC-02
    checklist_id: RTC-02
    description: SECONDS register increments from 0x58 to 0x59 after one RTC second
    expected: SECONDS == 0x59 after 32768 LFOSC ticks
  - id: CHK-RTC-03
    checklist_id: RTC-03
    description: Alarm A IRQ fires when calendar time matches alarm configuration
    expected: IRQ24 asserts at time 00:00:00
  - id: CHK-RTC-04
    checklist_id: RTC-04
    description: Alarm B fires at configured time independent of Alarm A
    expected: IRQ24 asserts at time 00:00:30; distinct from Alarm A event
  - id: CHK-RTC-05
    checklist_id: RTC-05
    description: Periodic wakeup IRQ fires at 8192-tick intervals; three consecutive events verified
    expected: Three IRQ24 assertions each separated by ~8192 RTC ticks

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-RTC checks PASS
  - BCD calendar arithmetic is correct across minute/hour rollover
  - Alarm A and Alarm B are independent (Alarm A cleared does not affect Alarm B)
"}
function.name: str_replace_editor

extras.new_content:
scenario_name: scenario_rtc_calendar
description: >
  Verifies the Real-Time Clock (RTC) of mspm33c321a_derivative. Tests cover
  BCD calendar set and read-back (seconds/minutes/hours/day/month/year),
  time advancement over one real-time second, Alarm A interrupt, Alarm B
  interrupt (independent of A), and the periodic wakeup timer interrupt.
checklist_ids:
  - RTC-01
  - RTC-02
  - RTC-03
  - RTC-04
  - RTC-05
test_names:
  - test_rtc_calendar_set_read
  - test_rtc_time_advance
  - test_rtc_alarm_a
  - test_rtc_alarm_b
  - test_rtc_periodic_wakeup
priority: P0
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

external_devices: []

rtc_params:
  rtc_clock_hz: 32768       # RTC driven by LFOSC at 32.768 kHz
  fast_sim_scale: 1000      # Speed-up factor for simulation (1 sim-second = 1000 RTC cycles)
  initial_time:
    seconds: 0x58
    minutes: 0x59
    hours:   0x23
    day:     0x28
    month:   0x02
    year:    0x26

stimulus:
  - step: 1
    action: release_reset
    description: Release reset; RTC in reset state
  - step: 2
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: RTC0
    register: ISR
    value: INIT_BIT
    description: Enter RTC init mode to allow calendar write
  - step: 3
    action: apb_write_rtc_calendar
    seconds: 0x58
    minutes: 0x59
    hours:   0x23
    day:     0x28
    month:   0x02
    year:    0x26
    description: Write BCD calendar time 23:59:58 on 28/02/2026
  - step: 4
    action: apb_write
    peripheral: RTC0
    register: ISR
    value: 0x00
    description: Exit RTC init mode; calendar starts running
  - step: 5
    action: apb_read_check_rtc
    fields: [SECONDS, MINUTES, HOURS, DAY, MONTH, YEAR]
    expected: [0x58, 0x59, 0x23, 0x28, 0x02, 0x26]
    description: Read back all calendar fields; verify BCD values match written data
  - step: 6
    action: wait_rtc_tick
    rtc_ticks: 32768
    description: Wait one RTC second (32768 LFOSC cycles in sim-scaled mode)
  - step: 7
    action: apb_read_check
    peripheral: RTC0
    register: SECONDS
    expected: 0x59
    description: Verify seconds advanced from 0x58 to 0x59
  - step: 8
    action: configure_rtc_alarm
    alarm: A
    seconds: 0x00
    minutes: 0x00
    hours:   0x00
    irq_enable: true
    description: Set Alarm A to fire at midnight (00:00:00)
  - step: 9
    action: wait_rtc_alarm_irq
    alarm: A
    irq_vector: 24
    timeout_rtc_ticks: 65536
    description: Wait for Alarm A IRQ (IRQ24) to fire when time reaches 00:00:00
  - step: 10
    action: check_irq
    irq_vector: 24
    description: Confirm Alarm A IRQ asserted
  - step: 11
    action: apb_clear_rtc_alarm_flag
    alarm: A
    description: Clear Alarm A interrupt flag
  - step: 12
    action: configure_rtc_alarm
    alarm: B
    seconds: 0x30
    minutes: 0x00
    hours:   0x00
    irq_enable: true
    description: Set Alarm B to fire at 00:00:30 (independent of Alarm A)
  - step: 13
    action: wait_rtc_alarm_irq
    alarm: B
    irq_vector: 24
    timeout_rtc_ticks: 98304
    description: Wait for Alarm B IRQ
  - step: 14
    action: check_irq
    irq_vector: 24
    description: Confirm Alarm B IRQ asserted independently
  - step: 15
    action: configure_rtc_wakeup
    interval_rtc_ticks: 8192
    irq_enable: true
    description: Configure periodic wakeup timer at 8192 RTC tick interval (~0.25 s)
  - step: 16
    action: wait_rtc_wakeup_irq
    count: 3
    irq_vector: 24
    timeout_rtc_ticks: 30000
    description: Verify wakeup IRQ fires three times at correct interval

checks:
  - id: CHK-RTC-01
    checklist_id: RTC-01
    description: All calendar fields read back matching BCD values after write
    expected: SECONDS=0x58, MINUTES=0x59, HOURS=0x23, DAY=0x28, MONTH=0x02, YEAR=0x26
  - id: CHK-RTC-02
    checklist_id: RTC-02
    description: SECONDS register increments from 0x58 to 0x59 after one RTC second
    expected: SECONDS == 0x59 after 32768 LFOSC ticks
  - id: CHK-RTC-03
    checklist_id: RTC-03
    description: Alarm A IRQ fires when calendar time matches alarm configuration
    expected: IRQ24 asserts at time 00:00:00
  - id: CHK-RTC-04
    checklist_id: RTC-04
    description: Alarm B fires at configured time independent of Alarm A
    expected: IRQ24 asserts at time 00:00:30; distinct from Alarm A event
  - id: CHK-RTC-05
    checklist_id: RTC-05
    description: Periodic wakeup IRQ fires at 8192-tick intervals; three consecutive events verified
    expected: Three IRQ24 assertions each separated by ~8192 RTC ticks

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-RTC checks PASS
  - BCD calendar arithmetic is correct across minute/hour rollover
  - Alarm A and Alarm B are independent (Alarm A cleared does not affect Alarm B)
